goal: Build a browser-based MVP for async interview practice with rubric-based feedback.
why_now: The team needs to validate whether people find structured async practice valuable before investing in live coaching or mobile apps.
in_scope: Create an interview session from a target role
Guide a user through a short sequence of practice questions
Generate rubric-based feedback after the session
Save and review past session summaries

decision_boundaries.decision: Choose the web framework and component library
decision_boundaries.notes: Framework choice may be made without asking as long as it supports the selected approach.

decision_boundaries.decision: Change the product from async practice to live coaching
decision_boundaries.notes: This would materially change the product concept.

constraints: The first release should be small enough to build as an MVP
The experience should work in a browser without native app installation
Feedback must be structured enough to be testable

acceptance_criteria.description: A user can start a practice session for a chosen role.
A completed session produces a rubric-based feedback summary.
A user can revisit a saved feedback summary.

open_questions: Should the first MVP accept only typed answers, or also simple recorded audio uploads?
Should resume context be pasted manually or uploaded as a file?
